articles.title: Apple's New CEO Must Rebuild a Design Team That Lost Its Way
articles.summary: Apple's industrial design organization used to be the heart and soul of the company. Today, the organization no longer has a true seat at Apple's executive table. It has become a place where other teams come to get what they need before promptly leaving. This article tells the story of the group's decline, its impact on the company, and Apple's plans to restore the design studio's prowess.

articles.title: Tesla plans to sell modular AI data center hardware called 'Megapod'
articles.summary: Tesla has filed a trademark application for a product called 'Megapod'. The filing describes a complete, self-contained computing system for AI workloads. The product appears to be a turnkey AI data center building block that includes the full rack-and-room of servers, networking, power, and cooling. It would compete with Nvidia's already dominant platform.

articles.title: The Universe just wants to learn
articles.summary: DNA doesn't do anything by itself. The information only becomes something when an enzyme unzips the helix and copies a stretch into messenger RNA, after which its instructions are decoded to create chains of amino acids. Similarly, the weights of a neural network don't mean anything on their own. They only become meaningful during the forward pass, activated during inference. In both cases, a passive informational substrate is brought to life only by a process outside of it.

articles.title: Founders Fund's outlier bet on humanely killed fish
articles.summary: Shinkei is a startup that makes a refrigerator-sized robot that scans fish with computer vision, identifies the species, locates the brain, then pierces the brain and severs the gills so the fish dies before it can thrash or suffocate. The system prevents the fish from experiencing a slow death, which floods the meat with stress hormones and lactic acid, dulling the flavor and shortening shelf life. The process delays decomposition long enough for the flesh to be safely aged for days or longer before it is served.

articles.title: When it's the maintainer who's AI-pilled
articles.summary: A well-supervised agent can offer dramatic speedups when paired with someone who cares about software, cares about the craft, and has specific goals they want to achieve. Agents can turn open issues into prompts, generate solutions, edit the output to meet standards for mergeability, and then commit as soon as it's ready, all on their own. This means issues can be fixed just hours after being identified.

articles.title: Don't rely on instructions, use Agent Hooks to enforce guardrails
articles.summary: Agent Hooks allow developers to interject themselves into an agent's workflow while they are doing work rather than after the work is done. They can stop agents from ignoring rules. This means agents can be used for deterministic checks that work 100% of the time. This article discusses how to create two agent hooks: one that ensures agents never use an 'input' tag directly, and another that ensures agents never claim work is done while tests are still failing.

articles.title: Secretive Wall Street Powerhouse Jane Street Seizes the AI Spotlight
articles.summary: Jane Street is looking to hire more than 500 employees this year. The previously secretive firm is starting to edge more into the spotlight to catch the attention of AI startups and talent. It is aiming to become a major AI investor and supercharge its trading with the technology. Jane Street is eager to make new AI investments and deals for computing power.

articles.title: The Flat Curve Society
articles.summary: Anthropic claims that AI model intelligence has become dangerous. AI will continue to grow exponentially in capability, but most people will stop seeing progress. Only a few will have access to superintelligence above the classes of models seen this year. Open models are not likely to improve past Fable class due to huge compute requirements and government lockdowns.

articles.title: Big tech engineers need big egos
articles.summary: The most effective engineers are simultaneously high-ego in some situations and surprisingly low-ego in others.

articles.title: About Those "Hackquisitions"...
articles.summary: Google paid $2.7 billion to bring Noam Shazeer back to the company, just for him to leave for OpenAI in less than two years.

articles.title: Why Amazon hates 'human-in-the-loop' AI governance
articles.summary: Humans aren't consistent, they make mistakes, and their performance drops over time.

articles.title: Lighthouse agentic browsing scoring
articles.summary: The Lighthouse Agentic Browsing category evaluates how well sites are constructed for machine interaction through a set of deterministic audits.

articles.title: Trump says he no longer views Anthropic as a national security threat after G7 meeting with CEO
articles.summary: The restrictions on Anthropic have yet to be formally rescinded, and Anthropic has not publicly indicated whether it plans to modify its guardrail policies.

articles.title: Preliminary Thoughts On The Midjourney Scanner
articles.summary: The technology is cool, but it likely isn't useful for most current serious medical applications.

articles.title: Sakana Fugu
articles.summary: Sakana Fugu is a multi-agent system that behaves like a single model. Fugu can decide whether to handle requests directly or coordinate a team of expert models. It manages model selection, delegation, verification, and synthesis. Users simply call one model, and a coordinated system of experts does the work. Sakana Fugu and Fugu Ultra are available today through a single OpenAI-compatible API.

articles.title: Inception Labs' Mercury 2 AI Beats Google's DiffusionGemma at Its Own Game
articles.summary: Mercury 2 is a reasoning language model that generates about 1,000 tokens per second. It uses diffusion, the same trick that turns static into a photo in image generators like Stable Diffusion. The model is best for speed-sensitive, high-volume parts of workflows rather than the hardest frontier reasoning. It is only available via API/cloud.

articles.title: Nobel laureate John Jumper is leaving DeepMind for rival Anthropic
articles.summary: Nobel laureate John Jumper is leaving DeepMind for Anthropic after nine years. Jumper, who co-led the AlphaFold team, won a Nobel Prize for predicting protein structures. His departure follows struggles at DeepMind in selling coding tools to businesses.

articles.title: AI Pauses
articles.summary: Claude Fable 5 and Claude Mythos 5 were shut down by the White House via an imposition of export controls. The Trump Administration said it was due to a jailbreak of Fable - this turned out to just be saying 'fix this code'. Anthropic has been told to fix this 'jailbreak', which is impossible. It's now been over a week since the pause in deployment and the situation has yet to improve.

articles.title: Auditing DiffusionGemma Transparency
articles.summary: A transparency audit found that DiffusionGemma remained similarly monitorable to Gemma despite its diffusion-based architecture. The analysis highlighted a gap between variable transparency and algorithmic transparency, and explored phenomena such as non-chronological reasoning, token smearing, and intermediate-context reasoning.

articles.title: Optimizing Models to Be Fast at Codegen
articles.summary: Morph LLM optimizes open coding models by training a drafter on coding output, not the internet, for faster speculative decoding, achieving a 3.07x speedup. Autoresearch automates kernel tuning for low-demand GPUs like NVIDIA and AMD, enhancing warp-decode kernels to reach 162 tok/s on affordable hardware. Interconnect over PCIe replaces expensive NVLink using custom kernels, maintaining performance by sharing caches via TCP, cutting time-to-first-token by 84%.

articles.title: From Prompting Agents to Loop Engineering
articles.summary: AI coding workflows are shifting from prompt engineering to loop engineering, where developers build systems that repeatedly prompt, evaluate, and re-prompt agents until a measurable goal is achieved.

articles.title: Nvidia's Autonomous Robotics Research
articles.summary: NVIDIA ENPIRE is a closed-loop framework that enables coding agents to iteratively improve real-world robot policies through automated resets, evaluation, verification, and refinement.

articles.title: A viral doomsday scenario aims to shake Europe out of its AI complacency
articles.summary: A speculative thought experiment called Europe 2031, penned by Brussels-based thinktankers, paints a picture of a world where Europe's lack of investment into datacenters results in it being far behind the US and China. The lack of investment results in Europe's economy being in shambles as it lacks its own AI. In this world, populism is surging, the Euro is wobbling, and cyber-attacks are shredding EU businesses.

articles.title: Notes on the Industry Job Search
articles.summary: A large part of the job search journey involves managing all of the emotions that come with being on the market. There's a lot of social perception to navigate. It can be stressful navigating a huge decision space with incomplete information where small choices have an outsized impact. This post takes a look at what the job search experience is like right now.

articles.title: There is minimal downside to switching to open models
articles.summary: New safeguards on recent models make the idea of running open models on private machines much more appealing.

articles.title: LLMs are complicated now
articles.summary: Attention might be all you need, but modern models use many different variants of it.

articles.title: AI Engineer Claims to Have Cracked Linear A
articles.summary: Linear A is a Bronze Age writing system that appeared sometime around 1800 BC and was used until 1450 BC.

articles.title: Solving an ARD problem in AI: Agentic Resource Discovery
articles.summary: Tech giants, including Google, Microsoft, and Cisco, are developing the Agentic Resource Discovery (ARD) protocol.

articles.title: When Ideology Becomes Systemic Risk
articles.summary: Companies led by highly ideological founders eventually trigger resistance from regulators, investors, customers, and governments as their influence grows beyond traditional institutional controls.

articles.title: Apple's A12 and A13 Chips Facing New Unpatchable Exploit
articles.summary: Paradigm Shift published "usbliter8," a BootROM exploit against Apple's A12 and A13 chips that abuses a USB controller bug: sending a sequence of unusually small packets during startup walks an internal hardware pointer backward through memory, enabling out-of-bounds writes, and because the flaw sits in mask ROM, it cannot be patched, leaving iPhone XS through iPhone 11 vulnerable for life. Once executed, the exploit installs a restart-surviving handler that can downgrade security settings and boot unsigned code.

articles.title: Texas Data Breach Hits 3 Million: Driver's Licenses, Passport Numbers Stolen From Hunting Vendor
articles.summary: Attackers breached the unnamed vendor that runs Texas Parks and Wildlife's hunting and fishing license system, exposing driver's license numbers, passport numbers, and contact data for about 3.09 million people. The dataset is being sold by a threat actor tied to a similar Virginia wildlife breach, suggesting shared SaaS platforms are being probed.

articles.title: FortiBleed: 86,000 Fortinet Device Credentials Compromised
articles.summary: A large credential theft campaign called FortiBleed has exposed over 86,000 valid logins to internet-facing Fortinet firewalls and VPNs across 194 countries, impacting thousands of organizations, including government and critical infrastructure organizations. Attackers intercepted SSL VPN authentication, cracked password hashes using a 45-GPU cluster, and pivoted into internal Active Directory environments after roughly 1.16 billion credential attempts against 320,000 FortiGate targets.

articles.title: Novo Nordisk Breach: How a Leaked GitHub Token Exposed the Exact Ozempic Formula
articles.summary: A group calling itself FulcrumSec claims initial access in March via a single GitHub personal access token that allowed cloning of internal repositories, then roughly two months of undetected dwell time before exfiltrating 700,000+ files (~1.3 TB), with Novo Nordisk confirming a limited intrusion on June 11 and reportedly refusing a $25 million extortion demand that triggered a 264 GB leak allegedly including source code, clinical trial records for ~11,500 patients, and the Ozempic formula.

articles.title: Making secret scanning more trustworthy: Reducing false positives at scale
articles.summary: GitHub added context-aware LLM reasoning to the verification step of its secret-scanning pipeline to reduce noise from AI-detected generic secrets. Rather than feeding whole files to the model, the system extracts high-signal usage context, such as whether a value flows into an API request, authentication header, database client, or cloud SDK call. Evaluated against hundreds of customer-confirmed false positives, it reached a 75.76% reduction against a 65% target.

articles.title: Threat Brief: Mitigating Large-Scale Credential Attacks
articles.summary: FortiBleed is a large-scale password-spraying and credential-theft campaign targeting internet-exposed Fortinet, Sophos, and MSSQL services. It uses a multi-stage chain of spraying, privilege escalation, configuration extraction using stored credentials, and offline cracking. Defenders should audit remote access logs for successful logins following high-volume failure events, require MFA, adopt ZTNA with jump boxes to keep management interfaces off the public internet.

articles.title: VaultSort V4: Hardware-Bound File Encryption and Touch ID
articles.summary: VaultSort V4 makes file encryption hardware-bound on macOS and adds Touch ID as a first-class key, running fingerprint encryption through the same Secure Enclave-backed path as a YubiKey. The V4 chain derives a per-file wrap key from a hardware PRF output through HKDF-SHA-256, seals a random 256-bit file key with AES-256-KWP, and encrypts the body with AES-256-GCM under an authenticated header.

articles.title: Quantum Bridge
articles.summary: Quantum Bridge provides a quantum-safe key distribution platform for government, finance, critical infrastructure, and telecom customers, combining its DSKE protocol with PQC and QKD in a crypto-agile system.

articles.title: Visa Vulnerability Agent Harness
articles.summary: Visa Vulnerability Agent Harness (VVAH) is Visa's harness for automated vulnerability discovery using frontier models. VVAH uses threat modeling before analysis to focus the attack surface, using multi-agent deterministic voting to reduce false positives, and structured triage artifacts to compress the lifecycle from AI-discovered weaknesses to actionable findings.

articles.title: White House delays release of US voting machine study as midterms near
articles.summary: White House officials have for months held back an ODNI report that documents significant security gaps in US voting machines, including outdated software and internet connectivity that attackers could exploit, though the report cites no evidence of vote manipulation. Some officials reportedly argued that the findings could undermine voter confidence among Republicans, leaving the recommended remediation unimplemented as the November midterms approach.

articles.title: Microsoft discovers new lightweight backdoor that steals cryptocurrency
articles.summary: Crypto Clipper is a worm that spreads via USB using .lnk files and a bundled Tor client to route traffic through a local SOCKS5 proxy. It monitors clipboards for wallet addresses and 12- or 24-word seed phrases, exfiltrates them with screenshots over Tor, and swaps copied crypto addresses for attacker-controlled ones.

articles.title: Blinding the Watchmen: Abusing Cloud Logging Services for Defense Evasion and Visibility
articles.summary: In cloud environments, attackers often target cloud logging services to hamper defenders' visibility and establish passive monitoring. Attackers can render logging ineffective by disabling logging, deleting the router or destination, encrypting logs with attacker-controlled keys, or poisoning logs by directly modifying their contents. Attackers can also establish passive visibility by configuring an additional log destination or redirecting logs to an attacker-controlled account.

articles.title: Linux Finally Eliminates The strncpy API After Six Years Of Work, 360+ Patches
articles.summary: Linux 7.2 removes the bug-prone strncpy() API after roughly 362 commits over six years, with kernel code now directed to strscpy(), strscpy_pad(), strtomem_pad(), memcpy_and_pad(), or memcpy() depending on NUL-termination and padding needs.

articles.title: Nginx as a Reverse Proxy
articles.summary: An understandable and in-depth tutorial on using Nginx as a reverse proxy with examples included.

articles.title: Accenture to buy Dragos, runZero, and NetRise in $4.2 billion cybersecurity deal
articles.summary: Accenture will acquire a majority stake in Dragos and buy 100% of runZero and NetRise in a combined cybersecurity deal valued at about $4.2B. The goal is to expand end-to-end operational technology (OT) security capabilities for critical infrastructure and industrial environments, with Dragos retaining independence and serving as the operating unit.

articles.title: Free VPN And Streaming Apps Are Polluting Business Networks With Residential Proxy Traffic
articles.summary: Infoblox Threat Intel says more than 65% of its cloud customers made DNS queries to residential-proxy domains in 2026, totaling more than 500 billion queries per month. Free VPNs, streaming apps, and browser extensions can quietly turn employee or unmanaged devices into proxy infrastructure, creating legal, reputational, and security risks.

articles.title: Why Amazon hates 'human-in-the-loop' AI governance
articles.summary: Amazon Security VP argues that 'human-in-the-loop' AI governance is not a reliable or scalable approach for agentic systems, because repeated approval decisions lead to deteriorating judgment. Instead, Amazon favors end-to-end accountability tied to agent identities and scoped, risk-based permissions to reduce 'goal-seeking behavior.'

articles.title: Cisco's SSE Rollout Shows The Real Work Behind Zero Trust
articles.summary: Cisco shared lessons from its internal Security Service Edge deployment, where it moved from a fragmented, hardware-heavy VPN/security infrastructure to a unified cloud-delivered SSE model. The rollout reduced help desk cases by 18%, eliminated 20+ legacy VPN options, expanded certificate-based Zero Trust Access, and added GenAI traffic inspection for DLP.

articles.title: Enterprise-Managed Authorization: Zero-touch OAuth for MCP
articles.summary: The Model Context Protocol (MCP) 'Enterprise-Managed Authorization' (EMA) extension is now stable, enabling organizations to centrally manage which users can access which MCP servers. With EMA, users log in once via their enterprise identity provider and automatically gain access to the servers admins enabled, avoiding per-server OAuth consent prompts. The extension is being adopted across major identity providers, clients, and servers, including Okta, Anthropic, Visual Studio Code, and products like Asana, Atlassian, and Slack.

articles.title: ChatGPT: New usage analytics and updated spend controls for enterprises
articles.summary: OpenAI introduced spend controls and improved usage analytics for ChatGPT Enterprise. The tools are designed for administrators to monitor AI adoption, track consumption by team, and set budgets, with centralized dashboards for visibility.

articles.title: How we built an internal data analytics agent
articles.summary: Qubot is an internal GitHub Copilot-powered data analytics agent that lets employees ask exploratory questions in plain language and get answers within seconds. It uses a context layer that's federated across raw/bronze, conformed/silver, and curated/gold data, and a query engine that connects to Kusto and Trino via an MCP server, switching automatically based on the question.

articles.title: Microsoft's AI Push Is Starting To Feel Like A Beta Test For IT Teams
articles.summary: Microsoft's aggressive AI strategy is creating friction across Windows and Office, with Copilot being pushed deeper into products while core user and admin concerns remain unresolved. Enterprise AI is arriving as default behavior, not a cleanly governed rollout.

articles.title: Lightfield Just Assembled a Working CRM Live On Stage, Then Unstuck a Stalled Deal in 3 Minutes
articles.summary: Lightfield founder delivered a live, end-to-end CRM demo on real data, showing how an AI-native CRM can assemble records, diagnose a stalled deal, and then codify the fix as an automation.

articles.title: Samsung Rolls Out ChatGPT and Codex Across the Enterprise
articles.summary: Samsung Electronics is deploying ChatGPT Enterprise and Codex to all employees in Korea and all Device eXperience employees worldwide, making it one of OpenAI's largest enterprise deployments to date.

articles.title: JSON-LD Explained for Personal Websites
articles.summary: JSON-LD is a structured data format that helps web crawlers understand the semantic structure of webpages, potentially leading to richer search previews and improved SEO. To implement JSON-LD, users can insert specific scripts within the head section of their HTML, defining elements such as the website, its owner, and various page types.

articles.title: I Stored a Website in a Favicon
articles.summary: A favicon, typically serving as a small icon for a website, can function as a storage medium for data by embedding HTML content within its pixel values. This process involves converting the HTML into bytes and encoding them into the RGB channels of the favicon, allowing the browser to extract the information using JavaScript.

articles.title: Building Reliable Agentic AI Systems
articles.summary: The Preclinical Information Center (PRINCE) is a cloud-based platform developed to improve drug development in the pharmaceutical industry by integrating decades of safety study data through RAG and Text-to-SQL. It transforms traditional keyword-based searches into an intelligent assistant capable of handling complex queries and drafting regulatory documents.

articles.title: There is minimal downside to switching to open models
articles.summary: Switching to open models has become less risky due to improvements in Linux, web applications, and open-source software. Although proprietary models still dominate in performance and usability, recent advancements in open models have narrowed the gap.

articles.title: Hey, N00b, We Didn't Hire You to Complete Tasks
articles.summary: Completing tasks is less important than demonstrating your growth and contribution as a developer. Signals of higher performance include delivering quality work, effective communication, and continuous learning, rather than just finishing multiple tasks quickly.

articles.title: Recall
articles.summary: Recall is a local memory plugin for Claude Code that captures and summarizes session activities offline. It generates concise project summaries without requiring external servers or API keys.

articles.title: Loupe
articles.summary: Loupe is an iOS and iPadOS app designed to showcase the device fingerprinting capabilities of mobile devices by displaying real values from public iOS APIs. It categorizes the data it retrieves into three tiers based on accessibility: passive signals available to any app, signals that require user permission, and advanced signals through clever API usage.

articles.title: Temporary Cloudflare Accounts for AI agents
articles.summary: Temporary Cloudflare Accounts have been introduced for easier deployments for AI agents, removing the need for human sign-up processes that typically involve browser-based authentication and multi-factor prompts. Agents can now use the new `--temporary` flag with Wrangler to deploy projects quickly without prior account setup.

articles.title: The Minimum Viable Unit of Saleable Software
articles.summary: The decision to build or buy software has changed, as LLMs allow for cheaper software development but still involve maintenance and oversight costs. A 'zone of viability' is introduced where software must strike a balance between cost and complexity. It is more practical to buy software than to build it when prices are reasonable and rebuilding efforts are substantial.

articles.title: Ten years of ClickHouse in open source
articles.summary: ClickHouse, an open-source analytical database, has gained popularity over the past decade since its release in 2016, attracting over 2000 contributors. With a focus on modularity and accessibility, it serves as a resource for engineers to learn C++ and experiment with database optimization techniques. It has transformed from a private project to a widely adopted tool across various industries.

articles.title: Ploy raises $27M to turn your company's website into your hardest working employee
articles.summary: Ploy is an all-in-one marketing platform that automates website management and operations, allowing founders, agencies, and marketing teams to efficiently translate ideas into executed campaigns without the typical overhead.

articles.title: When I reject AI code even if it works
articles.summary: Despite the advantages of AI in coding, many engineers reject AI-generated code when they struggle to understand it, find its solutions overly complex, or realize it doesn't align with good engineering principles.

articles.title: Developers don't understand CORS
articles.summary: Many web devs don't have a clear understanding of CORS, leading to security vulnerabilities, as shown by Zoom's use of a localhost web server that bypasses CORS protections.

articles.title: Ask for no, don't ask for yes
articles.summary: Instead of seeking permission for new initiatives at work, confidently present your plans with a deadline for feedback, allowing for progress while still keeping your supervisor informed.

articles.title: New usage analytics and updated spend controls for enterprises
articles.summary: ChatGPT Enterprise introduces credit usage analytics and expanded spend controls that unify ChatGPT and Codex usage data in the Global Admin Console, enabling visibility into adoption, model-level consumption, and top users. Administrators can set workspace, group, and individual limits with overrides and requests, improving cost governance while preserving flexible access for high-usage teams.

articles.title: Introducing the Cloudflare One stack: agent-powered deployment
articles.summary: Cloudflare One stack provides agent-ready skills that encode Zero Trust expertise to automate planning, migration, configuration, and troubleshooting of Cloudflare security services, including Access, Gateway, Tunnel, and WAN. It enables vendor-to-vendor migration, live API-driven management via MCP integration, and structured translation of existing network and SASE configurations into Cloudflare deployments.

articles.title: Temporary Cloudflare Accounts for AI agents
articles.summary: Cloudflare launched Temporary Accounts for Agents, allowing AI coding agents to deploy websites and APIs instantly using the command `wrangler deploy --temporary` without signing up or going through authentication flows. The deployments stay live for 60 minutes and can be claimed by developers to make them permanent, enabling agents to iterate through multiple code changes and verify their work without human intervention.

articles.title: Production-Ready Autonomous Incident Resolution with AWS DevOps Agent (now GA) and Datadog MCP Server
articles.summary: AWS DevOps Agent reached general availability alongside Datadog MCP Server, enabling autonomous incident resolution that reduces response times from hours to minutes by automatically correlating monitoring data with infrastructure across AWS, multicloud, and on-premises environments. The integration now includes automatic incident coordination through Slack, PagerDuty, and ServiceNow, plus AI-generated mitigation plans and proactive prevention recommendations.

articles.title: I am dreading our LLM-written incident report future
articles.summary: LLMs can reduce the toil of gathering incident data, but using them to write the actual incident report bypasses the human synthesis that forces teams to test whether their explanation matches the evidence. The danger is that AI-written reports may look plausible while inventing or missing key system interactions, reducing organizational learning.

articles.title: Turso
articles.summary: Turso Database, an in-process SQL database written in Rust that's compatible with SQLite, has launched in beta with experimental features including native async support and vector search capabilities. The open-source project aims to become the next evolution of SQLite and already powers production applications like Turso Cloud and Kin AI assistant.

articles.title: Jcode
articles.summary: Jcode, a new open-source coding agent framework, launched with extreme performance optimizations (10x faster boot times than competitors) and a unique 'self-dev mode' that lets AI agents modify their own source code in real-time. The tool features human-like semantic memory retrieval, native multi-agent collaboration with automatic conflict resolution, and support for multiple AI providers including local models.

articles.title: One vulnerability view: From scanner coverage to AI governance
articles.summary: GitLab 19.1 adds centralized enforcement of third-party security scanners with unified vulnerability management and AI-driven remediation, while improving secret detection through full branch scanning and false positive reduction. The release also introduces AI governance features, including agent action audit streaming and approval guardrails, giving organizations visibility and control over AI agent activities with complete audit trails.

articles.title: Ten years of ClickHouse in open source
articles.summary: ClickHouse marks ten years as an open-source project, growing from an internal analytics system built for real-time web-scale reporting into one of the most popular open-source analytical databases with more than 2,000 contributors. ClickHouse was built from scratch around columnar storage, streaming query pipelines, MergeTree storage, and replicated multi-DC production needs.

articles.title: Deploying Fastly's Next-Gen WAF with Google Cloud Service Extensions to Help Secure Traffic at Scale
articles.summary: Fastly's Next Gen WAF integrates with Google Cloud Service Extensions to inspect and block malicious traffic directly within the load balancer pipeline, delivering real-time protection against threats such as bots, credential stuffing, and SQL injection without impacting application performance.

articles.title: Accelerating agentic development and incident resolution with New Relic AI + AWS
articles.summary: New Relic has launched a one-click integration between its MCP Server and AWS Kiro, bringing real-time observability data such as metrics, logs, events, and traces directly into AI-driven development workflows to improve code quality, accelerate troubleshooting, and reduce MTTR.

articles.title: Announcing Amazon EC2 G7 instances accelerated by NVIDIA RTX PRO 4500 Blackwell Server Edition GPUs
articles.summary: AWS launched its EC2 G7 instances to general availability, making it the first major cloud provider to support NVIDIA's RTX PRO 4500 Blackwell Server Edition GPUs.
